clue: Every last Wednesday of August this small Spanish town hosts the world's biggest food fight, where about 20,000 people pelt each other with roughly 145,000 kilograms of squashed tomatoes.
This Finnish town has hosted the Wife Carrying World Championship since 1992, where the grand prize is the wife's weight in beer.
Every year on this English hill people hurl themselves down a brutally steep slope chasing a runaway wheel of Double Gloucester cheese that can hit 70 mph.
This Russian village is the coldest permanently inhabited place on Earth, where the temperature once dropped to about minus 90 degrees Fahrenheit.
This Thai city holds an annual Monkey Buffet Festival, laying out nearly two tons of fruit and vegetables on lavish tables for its 2,000 resident macaques.
During this Italian city's centuries-old carnival, teams re-enact a medieval revolt by pelting each other with over 900 tons of oranges in the largest food fight in Italy.
Because its permafrost stops corpses from decomposing (some 1918 flu victims never rotted), this Norwegian Arctic town closed its graveyard and now sends the dying and the dead to the mainland.
To promote tourism, Boring, Oregon and Bland, Australia teamed up with this dreary-named Scottish village to form a 'League of Extraordinary Communities' dubbed the Trinity of Tedium.
Software billionaire Larry Ellison, the founder of Oracle, bought about 98 percent of this entire Hawaiian island in 2012 for roughly 300 million dollars.

This roughly 2,060-square-km strip of desert between Egypt and Sudan is one of the only habitable places on Earth that no country will claim, because claiming it means giving up a more valuable region next door.
In this pair of tangled Belgian-Dutch border towns, the frontier runs straight through houses and shops, in one case splitting a front door between two countries.
A family declared their own tiny country on an abandoned WWII sea fort off England in 1967, and this self-proclaimed principality now funds itself partly by selling titles like Lord and Baron online.
The farthest point on land from any ocean, roughly 2,500 km from the nearest coast, sits in the Gurbantunggut Desert of this Chinese region near the Kazakhstan border.
Every August this small Kansas town holds a public 'twine-a-thon' where visitors add more string to keep growing its giant community ball of twine.
At the Hotel Arbez in this village straddling the France-Switzerland border, the international boundary runs right through the honeymoon suite's double bed.
